revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鉱泉分析法指針]]
'''Kōsen Bunseki Hōshishin''' (鉱泉分析法指針) is an administrative guideline established by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}}'s {{ill|Natural Environment Bureau|ja|自然環境局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Environment Bureau|1=Natural Environment Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612868}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/shishin_bunseki/top.pdf |title=鉱泉分析法指針 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} {{ill|Natural Environment Bureau|ja|自然環境局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Environment Bureau|1=Natural Environment Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612868}} |accessdate=2013-01-14}}</ref> The document is prepared by the {{ill|Central Research Institute for Hot Springs|ja|中央温泉研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Central Research Institute for Hot Springs|1=Hot Spring Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24865242}}, a public interest incorporated foundation.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.onken.or.jp/analysist.html#04 |title=業務実績 |publisher={{ill|Central Research Institute for Hot Springs|ja|中央温泉研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Central Research Institute for Hot Springs|1=Hot Spring Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24865242}} |accessdate=2013-01-14}}</ref> While the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} defines {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}, the guideline defines hot springs (鉱泉), their components, and classifications.
The guideline was first established in 1951 (Showa 26) by the former {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare (Japan)|ja|厚生省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Health and Welfare (Japan)|1=Ministry of Health and Welfare|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841449}}. Major revisions were made in 1957 (Showa 32), when Japanese names for substances were replaced with {{ill|IUPAC nomenclature|en|IUPAC nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IUPAC nomenclature|qq=}}, creating the old and new classifications for hot springs. Subsequent revisions took place in 1997 (Heisei 9), 2002 (Heisei 14), and 2014 (Heisei 26). In 1982 (Showa 57), the definition of therapeutic hot springs was reviewed.
== Definition of Hot Springs == {{seealso|Hot spring}} The guideline defines hot springs as follows: {{Cquote|Hot springs are defined as groundwater that is discharged to the surface, containing significant amounts of solid, gaseous, or special substances, or having a temperature significantly higher than the average annual temperature of the surrounding area.}} The {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} includes not only hot springs as defined above but also steam and other gases (excluding hydrocarbons) from the ground.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/point/ |title=温泉の保護と利用 温泉の定義 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} {{ill|Natural Environment Bureau|ja|自然環境局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Environment Bureau|1=Natural Environment Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612868}} |accessdate=2013-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} Article 2</ref>
The guideline distinguishes hot springs from ordinary water based on the following thresholds: ; Definition of Hot Springs (Distinction from Ordinary Water) :# Temperature: At least 25°C when extracted at the source. :# Substances (at least one of the following): {| class="wikitable" ! Substance !! Minimum Concentration (per 1 kg) |- | Dissolved substances (excluding gases) || Total ≥ 1000 mg |- | Free {{ill|carbon dioxide|en|Carbon dioxide|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide|qq=}} (CO<sub>2</sub>) || ≥ 250 mg |- | {{ill|Lithium|en|Lithium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lithium|qq=}} ions (Li<sup>+</sup>) || ≥ 1 mg |- | {{ill|Strontium|en|Strontium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Strontium|qq=}} ions (Sr<sup>2+</sup>) || ≥ 10 mg |- | {{ill|Barium|en|Barium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Barium|qq=}} ions (Ba<sup>2+</sup>) || ≥ 5 mg |- | Total {{ill|iron|en|Iron|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron|qq=}} ions (Fe<sup>2+</sup> + Fe<sup>3+</sup>) || ≥ 10 mg |- | {{ill|Manganese|en|Manganese|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manganese|qq=}} (II) ions (Mn<sup>2+</sup>) || ≥ 10 mg |- | {{ill|Hydrogen|en|Hydrogen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydrogen|qq=}} ions (H<sup>+</sup>) || ≥ 1 mg |- | {{ill|Bromide|en|Bromine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bromide|qq=}} ions (Br<sup>-</sup>) || ≥ 5 mg |- | {{ill|Iodide|en|Iodine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iodide|qq=}} ions (I<sup>-</sup>) || ≥ 1 mg |- | {{ill|Fluoride|en|Fluoride|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fluoride|qq=}} ions (F<sup>-</sup>) || ≥ 2 mg |- | {{ill|Arsenite|en|Arsenic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arsenite|qq=}} ions (HAsO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) || ≥ 1.3 mg |- | {{ill|Thiosulfate|en|Thiosulfate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thiosulfate|qq=}} (HAsO<sub>2</sub>) || ≥ 1 mg |- | Total sulfur (S) (corresponding to HS<sup>-</sup>, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub><sup>2-</sup>, H<sub>2</sub>S) || ≥ 1 mg |- | {{ill|Metaboric acid|en|Borax|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metaboric acid|qq=}} (HBO<sub>2</sub>) || ≥ 5 mg |- | {{ill|Metasilicic acid|en|Silicic acid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metasilicic acid|qq=}} (H<sub>2</sub>SiO<sub>3</sub>) || ≥ 50 mg |- | {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate|en|Bicarbonate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate|qq=}} (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>) || ≥ 340 mg |- | {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radon|qq=}} (Rn) || ≥ 74 Bq |- | {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium|qq=}} salts (as Ra) || ≥ 1 × 10<sup>-8</sup> mg |}
== Classification of Hot Springs == Hot springs are classified by temperature at discharge or extraction: {| class="wikitable" ! Classification !! Temperature (°C) |- | Cold Spring || < 25 |- | Low-temperature Spring || 25–34 |- | Medium-temperature Spring || 34–42 |- | High-temperature Spring || > 42 |}
Further classification is based on pH: {| class="wikitable" ! Classification !! pH Range |- | Acidic || < 3 |- | Slightly Acidic || 3–6 |- | Neutral || 6–7.5 |- | Slightly Alkaline || 7.5–8.5 |- | Alkaline || > 8.5 |}
== Therapeutic Hot Springs == Therapeutic hot springs are specifically defined for medical purposes based on temperature and composition, containing at least one of the substances listed above in specified concentrations.
== Notes == The guideline was revised in 2014 to update the definitions of therapeutic hot springs.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Analysis Report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Analysis Report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}} * {{ill|Onsen Bathing Instructor|ja|温泉入浴指導員|1=Hot Spring Bathing Instructor|1=Hot Spring Bathing Instructor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Bathing Instructor|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Bathing Instructor|qq=}} * {{ill|National Hot Spring Resort Areas|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Hot Spring Resort Areas|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421080}}
== Further Reading == * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/shishin_bunseki.html Ministry of the Environment: Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis (2014 Edition)]
{{Onsen}}
